purpose: FULL worker-agent progress-reporting protocol — the canonical worker reference. Defines the {tracker_root} vs {project_root} distinction, the progress file path, the 11-step quick-start checklist (read plan.json → write initial progress → query PKI → read upstream deps → progress through fixed stages → write on every transition → track files_changed[] incrementally → report deviations immediately → populate shared_context → annotate files → return structured summary), module index pointing to agent/worker/{progress_reporting,return_format,deviations,upstream_deps,sibling_comms}.md, when-to-read decision table, summaries for progress reporting (8 mandatory writes), deviations (CRITICAL/MODERATE/MINOR severity), upstream dependencies (4-step procedure), sibling communication, additional-context-directories (read-only), full PKI retrieval procedure (6 steps with manifest.json + annotations/{hash}.json + insights_index lookup), full PKI annotation contribution rules, return-format summary, and the 15-rule final summary. Read by every worker dispatched in FULL mode.
gotchas: {tracker_root} and {project_root} are different locations — code work happens in project_root, progress reporting goes to tracker_root. CWD is project_root, not tracker_root, so a relative `dashboards/` path would write progress into the target project. A hook blocks writes outside {tracker_root}/dashboards/.
plan.json is the canonical task spec — your dispatch prompt summarizes it but plan.json is source of truth. If missing or your task entry is absent, write a CRITICAL deviation and stop.
Server REJECTS progress files where dashboard_id field doesn't match the dashboard directory.
files_changed[] is NON-NEGOTIABLE and must be updated INCREMENTALLY (every create/modify/delete) — not at finalization. Hooks warn if files_changed is empty during implementing.

8 mandatory writes — skipping any is a failure: before starting work, after PKI/upstream reads, on every stage transition, on any deviation, on any error, on every file change, on completion, on failure.
Vague log messages (≥20 chars enforced; never 'Starting...', 'Done', 'Working on it') — hooks warn on sparse logs.
Minimum log counts per stage are hook-enforced: reading_context ≥1, planning ≥2, implementing ≥3, testing ≥4, finalizing/completed ≥5.
From implementing onward, milestones[] must not be empty.
Reserve `failed` for ZERO useful output — partial completion (80%+ done) uses `completed` with summary stating what remains blocked.
Always use the Write tool for progress files — not echo/cat shell commands.
PKI is OPTIONAL but recommended — never block on PKI reads, max 3-5 annotation reads per task, treat stale:true as suspect.
Annotations are append-friendly and lightweight — only annotate files you read DEEPLY, do not speculate.
Never write to a sibling worker's file — use shared_context for cross-task signaling instead.
Always capture timestamps live via `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"` — never guess or hardcode.
Include template_version (from dispatch prompt header) in every progress write.
patterns: Fixed stage sequence: reading_context → planning → implementing → testing → finalizing → completed | failed.
Atomic full-file writes — worker is sole owner of {task_id}.json, no read-modify-write cycle needed.
Three-severity deviation classification: CRITICAL (changes downstream contracts, may trigger replanning), MODERATE (different approach, same outcome), MINOR (cosmetic).
Upstream dependency 4-step procedure: read upstream progress files → extract critical info → adapt approach → log findings.
PKI retrieval 6-step flow: check manifest.json → identify task files → look up via files object + domain_index/tag_index → read 3-5 annotation files → check insights_index → log what was consumed.
Annotations schema: per relative-path key with optional gotchas/patterns/conventions string arrays.
Structured return: STATUS / SUMMARY / FILES CHANGED / EXPORTS / DIVERGENT ACTIONS — quantified one-line summaries.
Hub-and-modules layout: this file is the index/quick-start, agent/worker/*.md hold detailed procedures.
conventions: Module Index table at top mapping File → What It Covers.
When-to-Read decision table mapping Moment → Module to Read.
Numbered Quick Start Checklist (steps 0-11) followed by per-topic Summary sections.
Final 'Rules Summary' is a numbered list of NON-NEGOTIABLE rules with hooks-enforce annotations.

depended_by: agent/_commands/p_track_execution.md (Step 14 worker prompt template — INSTRUCTION MODE: FULL)
agent/instructions/tracker_multi_plan_instructions.md (child masters embed this path in their worker dispatches)
